title: Zuri's Playground Friends
synopsis: Zuri visits a new playground and learns how to make friends through kindness, sharing, and including others in play. When she sees other children playing alone, she discovers the joy of bringing everyone together.

pages.text: Zuri walked to the new playground with her mom. She had never been here before. The swings were tall and the slide was very big. There were lots of children playing and laughing. Zuri felt a little nervous. She didn't know anyone here.

illustration.description: Zuri with natural coily hair in an afro with a flower clip, deep brown skin, bright eyes standing at the entrance of a colorful playground with her mother, looking at swings and slides with diverse children playing in the background
illustration.alt: Zuri and her mom arriving at a bright, colorful playground

pages.text: "Go have fun, sweetie," said Mom. "I'll be right here on the bench." Zuri looked around. Some kids were on the swings. Others were going down the slide. Everyone seemed to have friends to play with. Zuri wished she had a friend too.

illustration.description: Zuri with natural coily hair in an afro with a flower clip, deep brown skin, bright eyes standing alone while diverse children play together on swings and slides in a bright, sunny playground with primary colors
illustration.alt: Zuri watching other children play while standing alone

pages.text: Zuri walked over to the sandbox. A boy with red hair was building a castle by himself. He looked sad. "Hi," said Zuri. "I'm Zuri. What are you making?" The boy looked up and smiled. "I'm Sam. I'm trying to build a castle, but it keeps falling down."

illustration.description: Zuri with natural coily hair in an afro with a flower clip, deep brown skin, bright eyes talking to a red-haired boy named Sam in a sandbox with a partially built sand castle, bright playground background with primary colors
illustration.alt: Zuri meeting Sam at the sandbox

pages.text: "Can I help you?" asked Zuri. Sam's face lit up. "Yes, please!" Together, they scooped sand and patted it down. Zuri helped Sam make the walls stronger. Soon they had built the best castle in the sandbox. "Thank you, Zuri!" said Sam. "You're a great friend."

illustration.description: Zuri with natural coily hair in an afro with a flower clip, deep brown skin, bright eyes and Sam working together building a tall sand castle in the sandbox, both smiling happily in a bright sunny playground setting
illustration.alt: Zuri and Sam building a sand castle together

pages.text: While playing with Sam, Zuri noticed a girl sitting alone on a bench. The girl had long black hair and was watching the other children play. She looked lonely. "Sam," said Zuri, "let's ask that girl to play with us." Sam nodded. "Good idea!"

illustration.description: Zuri with natural coily hair in an afro with a flower clip, deep brown skin, bright eyes and Sam looking toward a girl with long black hair sitting alone on a bench, playground with swings and slides in bright primary colors in the background
illustration.alt: Zuri and Sam noticing a girl sitting alone

pages.text: They walked over to the girl. "Hi! I'm Zuri and this is Sam. What's your name?" "I'm Maya," said the girl quietly. "Do you want to play with us?" asked Sam. Maya's eyes sparkled. "Really? Yes, I would love to play!" The three new friends ran back to the sandbox together.

illustration.description: Zuri with natural coily hair in an afro with a flower clip, deep brown skin, bright eyes, Sam, and Maya walking together toward the sandbox, all smiling, in a bright joyful playground with primary colors and white space
illustration.alt: Zuri, Sam, and Maya walking together as new friends

pages.text: Maya had great ideas for their sand castle. She showed them how to make a moat around it. Soon, two more children came over. "Can we play too?" asked a boy named Jake. "Of course!" said Zuri. "The more friends, the better!"

illustration.description: Zuri with natural coily hair in an afro with a flower clip, deep brown skin, bright eyes with Maya, Sam, and two other diverse children all working together on an elaborate sand castle with a moat in bright playground setting
illustration.alt: Five children working together on a large sand castle

pages.text: Now they had a big group of friends. They took turns going down the slide. They pushed each other on the swings. They played tag around the playground. Zuri had never had so much fun! "This is the best day ever!" she laughed.

illustration.description: Zuri with natural coily hair in an afro with a flower clip, deep brown skin, bright eyes playing tag with diverse group of children on a bright sunny playground with swings, slides, and lots of laughter and joyful energy
illustration.alt: Zuri and friends playing tag together on the playground

pages.text: When it was time to go home, all the friends were sad to say goodbye. "Can we play again tomorrow?" asked Maya. "Yes!" said Zuri. "Let's meet here every day!" They all promised to come back and play together again.

illustration.description: Zuri with natural coily hair in an afro with a flower clip, deep brown skin, bright eyes and her diverse group of friends in a circle holding hands saying goodbye at the playground with bright colors and happy expressions
illustration.alt: The friend group saying goodbye in a circle

pages.text: On the way home, Zuri told her mom about her new friends. "I'm so proud of you," said Mom. "You were kind and included everyone. That's how the best friendships begin." Zuri smiled. She couldn't wait to see her friends again tomorrow. The playground was much more fun with friends!

illustration.description: Zuri with natural coily hair in an afro with a flower clip, deep brown skin, bright eyes walking home with her mother, both smiling, with the colorful playground visible in the background under a bright sunny sky
illustration.alt: Zuri walking home happily with her mom

metadata.illustrationStyle: bright sunny playground, diverse kids playing, primary colors, white space, joyful energetic, inclusive, park swings slides, laughter, friendship activities

questions.question: How did Zuri feel when she first arrived at the new playground?
choices: nervous because she didn't know anyone
questions.explanation: Zuri felt nervous because she had never been to this playground before and didn't know any of the children there.

questions.question: What was Sam trying to build in the sandbox?
questions.explanation: Sam was trying to build a sand castle, but it kept falling down until Zuri helped him make it stronger.

questions.question: Why did Zuri and Sam invite Maya to play?
choices: because Maya looked lonely sitting by herself
questions.explanation: Zuri noticed that Maya was sitting alone on a bench and looked lonely, so she and Sam decided to invite her to play.

questions.question: What did Maya teach the group about sand castles?
choices: how to make a moat around the castle
questions.explanation: Maya showed them how to make a moat around their sand castle, which made it even better.

questions.question: What did Zuri's mom say she was proud of?
choices: that Zuri was kind and included everyone
that Zuri built the best castle
questions.explanation: Zuri's mom was proud that she was kind to others and included everyone, which is how the best friendships begin.
